content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

April 27, 1978

78-04-066

Mr. Samuel M. Winston, Jr.
2100 Greenbrier Lane
Eureka, California 95501

Dear Mr. Winston:

Thank you for your letter of March 23, 1978, requesting advice with respect to your obligations under the Political Reform Act. Since the questions raised in your letter do not involve an interpretation of unsettled areas of law, the Commission will not issue a formal opinion. However, I hope that the following comments, issued pursuant to Government Code §83114 as informal advice, will be helpful.

Government Code §87100 provides:

No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest.

Government Code §87103 provides, in relevant part:

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of §87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

(c) Any source of income, other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value received by or promised to the public official within twelve months prior to the time when the decision is made;....

[illegible]

Samuel M. Winston, Jr.
April 26, 1978                                                    Page Two


     You stated in your letter that you are a one-quarter
owner in the Carson Block Building.  Therefore, it would
appear that you have an investment worth $1,000 or more in
the Carson Block Building.  In addition, it appears that
you may have an interest in real property in the Building
and that the Building is a source of income to you.  Because
your letter does not state the amount of the lease, we are
unable to determine whether you have a "material financial
interest" in the Building.  I have enclosed a copy of
Commission Regulation 2 Cal.Adm. Code §18702, which sets
forth the standards for determining whether a decision
will have a material effect on an official's financial
interest.

     Assuming that you do have a material financial interest,
§87100 would require you to disqualify yourself from making
or participating in the making of the decision before the
County Planning Department.  Accordingly, if you have dis-
qualified yourself from participating in any discussions or
decisions regarding the lease, it would appear that you
have satisfied your obligations under the Act.

     I hope that these comments have been responsive to your
questions.  Please excuse the delay in responding.  If we
may be of further assistance, please let us know.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Ted Prim, Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division


TP:gb
encl.

2100 Greenbrier Lane
Eureka, California  95501
March 23, 1978



Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Re:  Conflict Opinion Request

Gentlemen:

     I hereby request that you issue an opinion with respect
to my duties and functions as Assistant Planning Director for
Humboldt County.  The facts giving rise to the possible con-
flict are stated below and in the attached letters.

     The County Planning Department wishes to lease a portion
of an office building in which I own a one-quarter interest.

     I have not participated in any discussions whether or not
the property should be leased or its terms.  This matter has
been handled by other employees in the Planning Department and
Public Works Department for the County.  The matters pertaining
to the lease have been handled by one of my partners, Mr. Allan
McViciar, who is a local realtor.  As the letters attached will
indicate, I opposed the leasing of the property to Humboldt
County but I was outvoted by my partners.

     Your early opinion with respect to my responsibilities in
the above mentioned transaction would be appreciated as I have
no control over whether the lease is executed by the Humboldt
County Board of Supervisors or by my partners.  If it is a
conflict, what do I do and where do I stand?

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [illegible]
                                        Samuel M. Winston, Jr.

cc:  Richard Oglesby
     Public Works Department

2100 Greenbrier Lane
Eureka, California 95501
March 15, 1978


Carson Block Building
P.O. Box 122
Eureka, California 95501

Re: Leasing of Office Space at Carson Block Building, 3rd
and F Streets, to County of Humboldt

Gentlemen:

This is to advise you that as a County employee and a partner of
the Carson Block Building, I am not in favor of the pending lease
between the County of Humboldt and the Carson Block Building.
My reasons are as follows:

I was notified by County Counsel of a possible conflict of interest
on the pending lease. They also advised me to seek an opinion
from the County's District Attorney. I went to see Mr. Buffington
and showed him a copy of the Government Code, Chapter 7 (Conflict
of Interest) Article 1. Mr. Buffington reviewed the code and
told me he would give me an opinion within a week. More than a
week passed and no word came from Mr. Buffington, so I called him
and he would not give me a yes or no on the possible conflict.
He said that he did not think it was a conflict, however, he would
not advise me to go through with the lease.

I know that my interest in the building is only 25 per cent and
you as my partners have the controlling interest and can do what
you wish, but I am requesting that you do not go through with the
pending lease with the County of Humboldt.

Sincerely,

Samuel M. Winston, Jr.

cc: Allan McVicar
Ralph Matsen
James Turner

P.S. Government Code Section 87100. When influencing governmental
decision prohibited. No public official at any level of state or
local government shall make, participate in making or in any way
attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.

Memo.

COUNTY OF HUMBOLDT
DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS

DATE: March 1, 1978

TO: Bob London
Planning Department

FROM: Richard Oglesby
Real Property Services Division

SUBJECT: Re: Your Request to Rent Space in the Carson Block Building

As Sam Winston is one of the four owners of that building,
I talked with Deputy County Counsel John Cook to see if he
felt there would be a conflict of interest. He referred us
to Section 83114 of the Government Code. He feels under this
section Mr. Winston should write the Fair Political Practices
Commission and ask them for a ruling as to whether his interests
in the proposed building would be in conflict with his job as
a County official.

If you have further questions, please contact John Cook in
regard to this matter.

Until I hear further from you, I will not proceed with the
proposed lease.

[signature]
RICHARD OGLESBY

ERO/sda

cc: Stan Mansfield, Planning
John Cook, County Counsel
Sam Winston, Planning

RECEIVED
MAR 1 1978

HUMBOLDT COUNTY
PLANNING COMMISSION

CARSON BLOCK
P. O. BOX 122
EUREKA, CA 95501

March 17, 1978


Mr. Bob London
Humboldt County Planning Commission
520 E Street
Eureka, CA 95501

Dear Mr. London:

You are hereby advised that a meeting of the owners of the Carson
Block was held on March 17, 1978, at which time three members voted to
rent to the County of Humboldt and one voted against.

The person voting against renting to you was Mr. Winston, who has
1/4 interest in subject property.

Please understand that any matters pertaining to the office of the
building, including leasing, etc., is being handled by McVicar Realty and
Mr. Winston is taking no active interest in the building offices' activities.

Yours very truly,

Allan A. McVicar

AAMc/cec

sections.question: I hereby request that you issue an opinion with respect to my duties and functions as Assistant Planning Director for Humboldt County. The County Planning Department wishes to lease a portion of an office building in which I own a one-quarter interest. I have not participated in any discussions whether or not the property should be leased or its terms. This matter has been handled by other employees in the Planning Department and Public Works Department for the County. The matters pertaining to the lease have been handled by one of my partners, Mr. Allan McVicar, who is a local realtor. As the letters attached will indicate, I opposed the leasing of the property to Humboldt County but I was outvoted by my partners. Your early opinion with respect to my responsibilities in the above mentioned transaction would be appreciated as I have no control over whether the lease is executed by the Humboldt County Board of Supervisors or by my partners. If it is a conflict, what do I do and where do I stand?
sections.question_synthetic: Does an Assistant Planning Director's one-quarter ownership interest in a building that the County Planning Department seeks to lease create a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100, and what are the official's duties and responsibilities in this situation?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter dated March 23, 1978, not an FPPC response. The document includes supporting correspondence from March 1, 15, and 17, 1978 providing context. No FPPC conclusion section exists. The requestor's name appears to be Sam Winston based on referenced communications, though the original request letter is unsigned.

embedding.summary: An Assistant Planning Director for Humboldt County seeks an FPPC opinion regarding whether his 25% ownership interest in a building proposed for lease to the County constitutes a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100. The requestor claims no participation in lease discussions and actively opposed the lease, which was approved by the other partners.
